IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 350 metres across 3 sections of the network. Completing this work in one visit will help reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place in land adjacent to Woodlands Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 7 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, A longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable.This work is taking place on land between Mill Road and Tilbury Green.

Steps.Message:
Hi David here again. I'm so sorry for how long power was off for. Our engineers have now restored power to all 270 customers in the Parkside area by diverting power around the unplanned faulty underground electrical equipment and due to this we have not needed to use the temporary generator.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve the issue safely. We apologise the outage lasted longer than anticipated and we appreciate your understanding.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by David at 05:33
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:

Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 1282 customers in the MK17 8 area by replacing some fallen overhead lines.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve the issue safely. We apologise the outage lasted longer than anticipated and we appreciate your understanding.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Kevin at 10:25

We know the time frame we initially estimated is now approaching. Our engineers are continuing their work to repair our overhead network in the MK17 8 area.

As our staff are working with live electricity, we need to wait until it is safe for them to stop and contact us with an update.

We're sorry you've been impacted by this, and we'll provide you an update as soon as possible. Sent by Kevin at 09:43.

Sent by Kevin at 08:57 - Our engineers are still working to restore your power.

We're sorry for the disruption this power cut may be causing. If you're worried about elderly or ill neighbours, we can offer help and support to our vulnerable customers during a power cut. Find out more here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR.

If you have any concerns, please call us on 08003163105

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 10:09 - Our staff have now managed to restore power back to the York Hill area again.

They have thoroughly investigated our network equipment in the area, but the underlying network fault has not yet developed to a point for it to appear on any of their diagnostic tests. As such, they have replaced our substation equipment to restore power as quickly as possible.

I'm sorry this affected your power again today. This may happen again before we're able to pinpoint the issue and fix it, but if you'd like to talk to us about anything or if your power isn't on, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again today. We believed all power was restored but it seems there may be a bigger underlying issue which is causing our substation equipment to operate repeatedly.

Our engineers are still on site and are now estimating to have all supplies back on between 13:30 and 14:30. Sent by Bev at 09:48.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 14:56 - We can now confirm that all power in the area should be back on. Our engineers had to unexpectedly turn off the power to 44 customers in the Mop End area to safely repair a fault on the network.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Talie at 14:11 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Mop End area for safety so they can repair a fault on the network. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 15:00 and 16:00, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.

Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected equipment fault that affected several thousand properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Joy at 15:17 - Our engineers have now restored power to several thousand customers in the Hove BN3 area, by by diverting power around the faulty equipment. We know power may have been off for a long time; we're sorry for any disruption this unplanned power cut caused.  

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations.

To talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 14:39 - Hi. I hope you're doing okay without your power; I understand how difficult a power cut can be during hotter weather and the additional worries this can bring.

Our network is built to be resilient to all types of weather, however high temperatures like today can have a similar impact to our equipment and cables as a storm, or high winds. 

For both our business customers and those at home, we will work as quickly and safely as possible to get the lights back on. If you need any more support or have any concerns, please call us on 08003163105 or tweet us @ukpowernetworks.

I'll let you know as soon as we have more news.

Sent by David at 22:47 - Hi David here from the messaging team. I'm sorry that power is still off this evening. Our engineers have just called in to advise us that they have run into complications with the ongoing work to the faulty underground electrical equipment which is now hindering our progress to restore your power.

This means its going to take us past our current time so we are now working towards between 23:30 and 00:30 to have your power back on.

We know how disruptive being without power can be, we are working as fast as we can. If you're worried about anyone who may be more vulnerable in your property who is affected by today's outage, please don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi, this is David - I wanted to let you know I have just taken over for the night shift, so I'll be here until tomorrow morning making sure you have the latest news from our staff.

I know that power being off may be especially worrying for you as we head later into the evening, but our field teams work 24 hours so I'll let know further news as soon as it comes in.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 20:56 - Thanks for your patience over today.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 20:50 - Our engineers have restored power to all 172 properties in the Horsham RH13 8 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have now diverted power.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi, this is David - I wanted to let you know I have just taken over for the night shift, so I'll be here until tomorrow morning making sure you have the latest news from our staff.

I know that power being off may be especially worrying for you as we head later into the evening, but our field teams work 24 hours so I'll let know further news as soon as it comes in.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 21:56 - We're sorry your power has gone off again. We are sending our engineers back to the Porchester Square W2 area to investigate why.

Currently, our time frame for power to be restored is between 01:00 and 02:00, but once our staff arrive on site and assess the situation, this time may change. As there have been other power cuts recently it's difficult to know what repairs will be needed to restore your power safely, so time frames we provide throughout have to be estimated.

The results of our engineer's tests on our network will let us know if our estimate needs to be adjusted, so we'll update you as soon as they have arrived on site and investigated.
